response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with 15+ years across web, mobile, and some systems programming, but the role demands principal-level hardware performance architecture expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU/SIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in this resume. His low-latency DLang backend work and brief C/C++ tooling experience are the closest touchpoints, but they are far removed from deep-learning workload analysis and silicon-level performance modeling. He meets the generic years-of-experience bar but not the domain requirements.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/product full-stack development, not performance architecture at the hardware level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload expertise
